notes: GOV.UK Approved Documents collection remains the primary source registry for England-first Approved Document pages.
Approved Document L 2026 exists as a separate GOV.UK publication; Codex must treat Part L pages as version-aware and must not hard-code outdated 2021/2022 assumptions as current for new work.
BSR higher-risk-building routing must remain separate from ordinary domestic building control routing.
Planning Portal is useful as a user-facing process source for application routes, but GOV.UK and legislation remain primary authority for statutory source blocks.

sources.title: Approved Documents collection
observed_claims: Approved Documents provide guidance on ways to meet the building regulations.
The collection lists Parts A, B, C, D, E, F, G, H, J, K, L, M, O, P, Q, R, S, T and Document 7.

sources.title: Approved Document L (2026)
observed_claims: Earlier versions continue to apply to buildings subject to previous regulatory standards.

sources.title: Building Safety Regulator strategic plan 2026 to 2027
observed_claims: BSR is the building control authority for higher-risk buildings in England.
HRBs are described as at least 7 storeys or at least 18 metres high with 2 or more residential units, or a hospital or care home.

sources.title: Planning Portal building control guide
observed_claims: Building regulations are minimum standards for design, construction and alterations to virtually every building.
Building control approval can be through local authority or registered building control approver routes.
